campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and the city's efforts to recruit and retain employees. The council also addressed public safety matters related to home security and announced that the Boogie Festival is happening this weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention efforts
Home security concerns
Boogie Festival this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 20, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council passed a resolution to identify certain properties in Saratoga and scheduled a public hearing for June 17, 2026, where residents can protest and the city will make a final decision on the matter.
keyTopics: Resolution passed to identify designated properties
Public hearing scheduled for June 17, 2026
Residents can submit protests before final approval

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026, to approve routine administrative matters and set up a municipal election for later that year. The council confirmed a new youth commissioner, approved a new housing development map, and authorized the town manager to sign several service agreements with vendors.
keyTopics: Youth Commissioner appointment confirmed by selection committee
November 3, 2026 municipal election scheduled
New housing development (Tract 10675 - Boulevard) final map approved
Town manager authorized to sign vendor service agreements including a 5-year deal with Accela, Inc.
Budget authorization for revenue and expenditures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 10, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for June 16 and June 23, 2026. The council reviewed which items to add to these agendas, set specific times for certain discussions, and checked whether proper public notice requirements were met. The council also discussed the Community and Economic Development Committee's work plan.
keyTopics: Planning the June 16, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planning the June 23, 2026 council meeting agenda
Setting time slots for specific agenda items
Community and Economic Development Committee work plan

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 9, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 9, 2026, that included a closed session to discuss hiring a new City Attorney, followed by a regular session where the council recognized Pride Month and Juneteenth with proclamations.
keyTopics: City Attorney hiring decision (closed session)
Pride Month proclamation
Juneteenth proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 16, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 16, 2026. The first was a closed session (details not publicly disclosed), followed by a study session focused on updating the community about the city's equity assessment efforts.
keyTopics: Community Equity Assessment Update
Closed session discussion (details withheld from public record)

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a study session to review how parks and recreational facilities are currently zoned and designated in Cupertino, and to make any necessary corrections to comply with California state law. The meeting also included a public comment period where residents could speak about any city matters not on the formal agenda.
keyTopics: Review of zoning designations for parks and recreational facilities
Corrections to existing parkland site protections
Compliance with state law requirements for recreational areas
Public comment period on non-agenda items (limited to 30 minutes)

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council is holding Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee meetings to discuss issues affecting neighborhoods around the stadium. The meetings are open to the public both in-person and online through Zoom.
keyTopics: Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee meetings
In-person and remote participation options available
Public comment submissions via email (stadiumrelations@santaclaraca.gov by 10 a.m. before meeting)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review housing and community development funding plans for 2025-2026, consider changes to how council vacancies are filled, and approve meeting minutes and schedules. Council members also submitted requests for new items to be added to future agendas.
keyTopics: Update to federal housing and community development funding plan (Consolidated Plan)
Proposed changes to the council vacancy ordinance
New workplan items requested by individual councilmembers
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 28, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss updates from various departments and committees, with major focus on improvements to the Foothills Nature Preserve and decisions about how to manage and fund the middle school athletics program going forward.
keyTopics: Foothills Nature Preserve improvements project
Future management and direction of middle school athletics program
Parks and Recreation Commission work plan for fiscal year 2027
Department reports and committee liaison updates
